setup.subtitle: Create the first administrator account.
setup.unavailable: Initial setup is no longer available because a user already exists.
setup.create_failed: Failed to create the admin account.
setup.username_invalid: Usernames can only contain letters, numbers, and underscores.

deck.columns_empty: All columns are hidden. Toggle a timeline back on from the rail.

airport.pageTitle: {name} Airport — a live map of this server
airport.title: {name} Airport
airport.subtitle: The last 24 hours of this server, run as a working airport. Every path matches the real wiring, and every number is real.
airport.sceneLabel: Live airport simulation of the server pipeline

default.title: {name} Airport
default.body: Every facility on this map is a real part of this server's fediverse pipeline. Hover anything to see which part it is.

cloudflare.title: Cloudflare gate
cloudflare.body: The edge. Every HTTP request from the fediverse and from browsers reaches this server through Cloudflare's network first.

checkin.title: Check-in
checkin.body: The rate limiter. It caps how often a single client can hit the API, so bursts never crush the server.

security.title: Security
security.body: Bot defense (Turnstile). AI crawlers and spam bots are turned away here; real people pass straight through.

passport.title: Passport control
passport.body: Authentication. Your login session or app's OAuth token is verified here before any write reaches the server.

gate.title: Aircraft stands
gate.body: The federation queue. A freshly published post waits here as a delivery job (QUEUE_FEDERATION) until the queue-consumer flies it out.

immigration.title: Immigration
immigration.body: Inbound HTTP-signature verification. Every activity that arrives in the inbox is checked to prove it really came from the server it claims.

baggage.title: Baggage claim
baggage.body: Remote media. Attachments that ride along with federated posts are fetched and served through the media proxy.

exit.title: Exit
exit.body: The timeline. A verified remote post leaves the pipeline here and appears in local feeds.

cargo.title: Cargo terminal (R2)
cargo.body: Object storage (R2 MEDIA_BUCKET). Every uploaded image, video, and audio file lives here — media never travels with the post itself.

announce.title: Announcements (streaming)
announce.body: The streaming service (a Durable Object). It pushes new posts and notifications over WebSocket to every connected client in real time.

tower.title: Control tower (ops)
tower.body: The queue-consumer worker. It processes the federation queue in order, signs activities, delivers them, and records success or failure per server.

dlq.title: DLQ holding apron
dlq.body: The dead-letter backlog (federation_dlq_parked). Deliveries that exhausted every retry park here until an admin replays or clears them — red means something is not landing.

deprwy.title: Departure runway 09L
deprwy.body: Federation out. The queue-consumer signs each ActivityPub activity and POSTs it to the destination server's inbox — straight out, not back through Cloudflare.

arrrwy.title: Arrival runway 09R
arrrwy.body: Federation in — this server's shared inbox. Activities that other servers send land here.

info.beaconBody: Another fediverse server this one federates with. Beacon size is how many posts arrived from it in the last 24 hours.

gate.title: Front gate & terminal
gate.desc: The front door between you and {title} — this very page came through it. New posts (departing passengers): {departures} in the last day.

federation.title: International routes (federation)
federation.desc: {arrivals} passengers arrived from {destinations} airports in the last day. New posts check in here for departure into the federated sky.
federation.delayedNote: These routes have accumulated delivery failures:

transfer.title: Transfer corridor
transfer.desc: {transfers} arriving passengers took the transfer corridor back to a departure gate in the last day. Boosts travel this way.

cargo.title: Cargo terminal (R2)
cargo.desc: {out} checked bags ({bytes}) entered the warehouse and {in} arriving bags reached baggage claim in the last day.

passport.title: Passport desk
passport.desc: {count} new passports were issued in the last day. A new account is a new passport.

tower.title: Control tower broadcast
tower.desc: When a new post lands, the tower announces it straight to connected screens. {total} announcements in the last day.

dlq.title: Grounded cargo yard
dlq.emptyDesc: The yard is completely empty. That's good news.
dlq.parkedDesc: {count} deliveries never made it and are parked in the yard. An admin can replay or clear them.

p1.lead: Every facility is a real component.
p1.body: This is the server's actual pipeline drawn as an airport: nothing on the map exists that isn't real wiring, and every real path is drawn. The aircraft are ambience; the numbers are not.

p2.body: Every request enters through the Cloudflare edge, then passes the rate limiter (check-in), bot defense (security), and authentication (passport control) before it can do anything — the exact order of the real middleware.

p3.body: Images and videos never travel inside a post. They live in R2 object storage (the cargo terminal) and move separately — uploads on the way out, remote attachments fetched through the media proxy on the way in.

p4.body: Posts from other servers land in the shared inbox (09R), have their HTTP signatures verified (immigration), and only then enter local timelines (the exit).

p5.body: A Durable Object holds a WebSocket to every open client and pushes new posts and notifications the moment they happen.

p6.body: The control tower is the queue-consumer: it works through the federation queue and logs delivery success or failure per server. Deliveries that exhaust all retries park in the dead-letter table (DLQ holding) for the admin to replay. Counts refresh about once a minute; no accounts, recipients, or content included.
